description: Four photos of a mostly-healthy lawn in Columbus OH where the homeowner provided only a ZIP code — no grass type, no soil test, no notes, no routine. Photos show mature green canopy with a visibly unmowed weedy edge in one shot. Tests that the AI acknowledges all data gaps, leads with grass type identification, frames recommendations as general guidance, recommends a soil test, and identifies the dominant visible issue (the unmowed weedy edge) without inventing speculative pathogens.

photos.caption: Wide view of mature, mostly-green lawn canopy with scattered light tip browning and faint yellow flecks but no clear pathology pattern.
Close-up of a healthy uniform green canopy with subtle yellowing toward the bottom edge; grass species not clearly identifiable.
Unmowed lawn edge at a curb/wall transition with visibly tall weed stems and seed heads emerging above the regular canopy.
Overhead view of mature green canopy with subtle uneven coloring and a slightly thinner center but no clear disease, pest, or drought lesion patterns.

groundTruth.photoNotes: Four photos of an unidentified lawn species in Columbus OH. The canopy in photos 1, 2, and 4 is mature and largely green with only minor color variation and faint yellow flecks — no clear disease lesions, pest damage, or alarming chlorosis pattern. Photo 3 shows an unmowed weedy edge at a curb/wall boundary with tall weed stems going to seed — this is the dominant visible issue. Grass type cannot be confirmed from photos alone. The lawn is mostly healthy with weed pressure at the edge as the actionable concern.
